content:
AUDIENCE:  
The reader is a senior business executive with a demanding schedule, limited time, and no technical or scientific background. They are familiar with basic business and communication concepts but likely have only a superficial understanding of how technology works. They care about efficiency, security, and protecting sensitive company information but don’t want to get bogged down in jargon or complex details. They are interested in practical benefits and clear, straightforward explanations. Overly technical language or abstract theories would confuse or bore them. A confident, concise, and approachable tone with relatable analogies will engage them best.

===BEGIN===  
Encryption is like a secret code that protects your online messages from being read by anyone except the intended recipient. When you send a message, encryption scrambles it into a jumble of characters that only the person with the right “key” can unlock and read. This process happens automatically and instantly, keeping your communication private even when it travels across the internet. Think of encryption as sending a locked briefcase—only the person with the key can open it and see what’s inside. This ensures that sensitive business information, personal details, and confidential conversations stay safe from hackers or unauthorized viewers. In simple terms, encryption is a trusted security tool that quietly guards your messages so you can focus on your work with peace of mind.  
===END===
